Combined Sewer Overflows - Part 1

Understanding the Challenge

Home Industries Proof Resources Contact Assets

What Are Combined Sewer Overflows?

In older cities, combined sewer systems collect both stormwater and sanitary wastewater in the same pipes. During heavy rainfall, the system can become overwhelmed, forcing untreated sewage into rivers and streams.

The Challenge

Water utilities must maintain large pumping systems to manage combined sewers. Equipment failures during peak flow events can be catastrophic. Samotics condition monitoring helps ensure these critical systems stay operational.

Key Infrastructure

The critical assets in combined sewer systems include:

  • Large centrifugal pumps
  • Motor-driven equipment
  • Gate mechanisms and controls
  • Backup generators

Next Steps

In part 2, we'll explore specific monitoring strategies for combined sewer infrastructure and how predictive maintenance prevents overflow events.
